video_title: Bad Influence: The Dark Side of Kidfluencing | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I was already bored halfway through the third episode and honestly prayed for the show to end as soon as possible. The main issue with this documentary, compared to other Netflix documentaries, is that there’s usually at least one person you can sympathize with. But in this case, all the moms seemed just as responsible as Tiffany.

If their kids had made the same amount of money or found success on YouTube, they probably wouldn’t even be in this documentary. They’re only here because their children didn’t “make it.” Where were their concerns when their 12-year-old daughters and sons were kissing each other on camera? Now they’re saying their children’s “careers” are destroyed—seriously? What career? Do they really believe they had one?

video_title: Bad Influence: The Dark Side of Kidfluencing | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Anyone else notice how Netflix docs ALWAYS seemingly push political agendas? 🤔 This one's is crystal clear: convice the masses that MORE LAWS, MORE TAX-PAYER FUNDED BUREAUCRATIC REGULATORY AGENTS, and MORE RED TAPE is the answer here, and it's not. Kids from vlogger families can be protected from financial exploitation through MANY proactive measures that DO NOT require our corrupt governmental bodies from getting involved (excluding instances of criminal behaviors warranting law enforcement intervention, of course....). Independent non-governmental consumer protection/"watchdog agencies" and the popularization/mainstreaming of industry-standard monetized payment requirements on the part of hosting platforms (i.e. Youtube) with respect to compensation of family vloggers who feature their children might be the better option here, in my opinion. Also, the mothers featured in this documentary DO NOT represent EVERYONE who chooses to showcase their family. These parents should be investigated for their own individual acts of crime (i.e. child-harm) - NOT used to emotionally manipulate the masses into BEGGING for governmental regulations. Just saying!!!

comment_text: Thankfully I hadn't heard of any of these people before watching this trailer, so I naturally became curious. Piper is only 17 years old and her channel was still very active until 3 months ago (I'm assuming she stopped posting knowing this documentary was coming out...) From the thumbnails and titles of her videos that I just saw in a 30 second span, I'm appalled and completely sickened. A 17 year old has been totally exploited and brainwashed by her mother into thinking it's okay for a CHILD to act in such ways and produce such content for the world to see 😱😪

video_title: Bad Influence: The Dark Side of Kidfluencing | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: I watch of the children youtube video with my kid sometimes. Some have millions of subscribers and millions of views per video. Their early videos usually fun, homemade, silly and the kids look like they really enjoy it. But then the newer video look so sleek and usually taken in luxury mansions with crazy expensive toys, luxury cars, designer clothes with upscale brand logo printed, elaborate birthdays with Limousine and eventually with younger siblings. The parents usually quit their job to become "full time youtuber" based on wikipedia. But, really, their kids are the ones actually working and whose lives are over exploited to provide a luxury living. One of a family youtube have kids who clearly going into pre teen, so they use their younger children to fill in to keep the views going I suppose. It's really sad to these kids. I was a nanny for children once and I look after a lot of my nieces and nephews, now I have a child of my own. I can say 100% these kids are forced as no child are willing to be on video all the time.
